title: nassella pfisteri
intro: Nassella pfisteri, also known as Pfister's needle grass, is a rare and endangered species of grass found in the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with Nassella pfisteri include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Nassella pfisteri are typically made by infusing the plant in hot water or olive oil, and it can be used as a tincture or applied topically to the affected area.
